translations.explanation: Quand un autobus scolaire affiche des feux rouges clignotants, toute la circulation dans les deux directions doit s'arrêter et céder jusqu'à ce que les feux cessent de clignoter.
choices: Toute la circulation dans les deux directions

text: At an intersection, a vehicle turning right has the right of way over:
explanation: A right turn typically has the right of way over a left turn from the opposite direction, but must still yield to straight-through traffic and pedestrians.

choices.text: Vehicles turning left from the opposite direction

translations.text: En una intersección, un vehículo dando vuelta a la derecha tiene el derecho de paso sobre:
translations.explanation: Una vuelta a la derecha típicamente tiene el derecho de paso sobre una vuelta a la izquierda desde la dirección opuesta, pero aún debe ceder el paso al tráfico que va derecho y a los peatones.
choices: Vehículos dando vuelta a la izquierda desde la dirección opuesta
Peatones en el paso de peatones

translations.text: 在路口，右转车辆拥有优于以下车辆的路权：
translations.explanation: 右转通常比对面方向的左转拥有路权，但仍需让行给直行交通和行人。

translations.text: Em um cruzamento, um veículo convertendo à direita tem preferência sobre:
translations.explanation: Uma conversão à direita tipicamente tem preferência sobre conversão à esquerda do sentido oposto, mas ainda deve dar preferência ao tráfego em frente e pedestres.
choices: Veículos convertendo à esquerda do sentido oposto

translations.text: À une intersection, un véhicule tournant à droite a la priorité sur :
translations.explanation: Un virage à droite a généralement la priorité sur un virage à gauche venant de la direction opposée, mais doit encore céder à la circulation allant tout droit et aux piétons.
choices: Les véhicules tournant à gauche de la direction opposée
Les piétons dans le passage pour piétons

text: When leaving a private driveway to enter a public road, you must:
explanation: Traffic on the public road has the right of way, and vehicles leaving private driveways must yield to all traffic.

choices.text: Yield to all traffic on the public road
Proceed immediately if no cars are visible
Only yield to traffic from the left

translations.text: Al salir de una entrada privada para ingresar a una vía pública, debe:
translations.explanation: El tráfico en la vía pública tiene el derecho de paso, y los vehículos que salen de entradas privadas deben ceder el paso a todo el tráfico.
choices: Tocar la bocina antes de ingresar
Ceder el paso a todo el tráfico en la vía pública
Proceder inmediatamente si no hay autos visibles
Solo ceder el paso al tráfico de la izquierda

translations.text: 从私人车道进入公共道路时，必须：
translations.explanation: 公共道路上的交通拥有路权，从私人车道驶出的车辆必须让行给所有交通。
choices: 让行给公共道路上的所有交通
如果看不到车辆立即通行

translations.text: Ao sair de uma garagem particular para entrar em via pública, você deve:
translations.explanation: O tráfego na via pública tem preferência, e veículos saindo de garagens particulares devem dar preferência a todo o tráfego.
choices: Dar preferência a todo tráfego na via pública
Prosseguir imediatamente se não há carros visíveis
Apenas dar preferência ao tráfego da esquerda

translations.text: En quittant une entrée privée pour entrer sur une route publique, vous devez :
translations.explanation: La circulation sur la route publique a la priorité de passage, et les véhicules quittant les entrées privées doivent céder à toute la circulation.
choices: Céder à toute la circulation sur la route publique
Procéder immédiatement si aucune voiture n'est visible
Céder seulement à la circulation venant de la gauche

text: Funeral processions have the right of way:
explanation: Legally formed funeral processions with proper identification have the right of way and should not be interrupted by other traffic.

translations.text: Las procesiones fúnebres tienen el derecho de paso:
translations.explanation: Las procesiones fúnebres legalmente formadas con identificación apropiada tienen el derecho de paso y no deben ser interrumpidas por otro tráfico.
choices: Una vez legalmente formadas e identificadas

translations.text: 送葬队伍拥有路权：
translations.explanation: 合法组成并有适当标识的送葬队伍拥有路权，不应被其他交通打断。

translations.text: Cortejos fúnebres têm preferência:
translations.explanation: Cortejos fúnebres legalmente formados e com identificação adequada têm preferência e não devem ser interrompidos por outros veículos.
choices: Uma vez legalmente formados e identificados

translations.text: Les cortèges funèbres ont la priorité de passage :
translations.explanation: Les cortèges funèbres légalement formés avec identification appropriée ont la priorité de passage et ne doivent pas être interrompus par l'autre circulation.
choices: Une fois légalement formés et identifiés

text: When must you yield to a pedestrian with a white cane or guide dog?
explanation: Pedestrians who are blind or visually impaired using white canes or guide dogs must be given the right of way at all times.

translations.text: ¿Cuándo debe ceder el paso a un peatón con bastón blanco o perro guía?
translations.explanation: Los peatones ciegos o con discapacidad visual que usan bastones blancos o perros guía deben tener el derecho de paso en todo momento.

translations.text: 何时必须让行给持白手杖或导盲犬的行人？
translations.explanation: 使用白手杖或导盲犬的盲人或视障行人在任何时候都必须被让行。

translations.text: Quando você deve dar preferência a pedestre com bengala branca ou cão-guia?
translations.explanation: Pedestres cegos ou com deficiência visual usando bengalas brancas ou cães-guia devem ter preferência em todas as situações.

translations.text: Quand devez-vous céder le passage à un piéton avec une canne blanche ou un chien-guide ?
translations.explanation: Les piétons aveugles ou malvoyants utilisant des cannes blanches ou des chiens-guides doivent avoir la priorité de passage en tout temps.

text: At a green light, you must still yield to:
explanation: Even with a green light, you must yield to pedestrians in the crosswalk and any vehicles still clearing the intersection.

choices.text: No one, you have complete right of way
Pedestrians and vehicles already in the intersection

translations.text: En una luz verde, aún debe ceder el paso a:
translations.explanation: Incluso con luz verde, debe ceder el paso a los peatones en el paso de peatones y cualquier vehículo que aún esté despejando la intersección.
choices: Nadie, usted tiene derecho de paso completo
Peatones y vehículos ya en la intersección

translations.text: 绿灯时，仍必须让行给：
translations.explanation: 即使是绿灯，也必须让行给人行横道上的行人和仍在路口内的车辆。
choices: 没有人，你拥有完全路权
行人和已在路口内的车辆

translations.text: No sinal verde, você ainda deve dar preferência a:
translations.explanation: Mesmo com sinal verde, você deve dar preferência a pedestres na faixa e quaisquer veículos ainda atravessando o cruzamento.
choices: Pedestres e veículos já no cruzamento

translations.text: Au feu vert, vous devez encore céder le passage à :
translations.explanation: Même au feu vert, vous devez céder aux piétons dans le passage pour piétons et aux véhicules qui dégagent encore l'intersection.
choices: Personne, vous avez la priorité complète
Les piétons et véhicules déjà dans l'intersection

text: When merging onto a freeway, the responsibility to yield belongs to:
explanation: The vehicle merging onto the freeway must yield and find a safe gap in traffic before entering.

translations.text: Al incorporarse a una autopista, la responsabilidad de ceder el paso pertenece a:
translations.explanation: El vehículo que se incorpora a la autopista debe ceder el paso y encontrar un espacio seguro en el tráfico antes de ingresar.
choices: El tráfico que ya está en la autopista

translations.text: 汇入高速公路时，让行责任属于：
translations.explanation: 汇入高速公路的车辆必须让行并找到安全间隙后再进入。
choices: 已在高速公路上的交通

translations.text: Ao entrar em uma rodovia, a responsabilidade de dar preferência pertence a:
translations.explanation: O veículo entrando na rodovia deve dar preferência e encontrar uma brecha segura no tráfego antes de entrar.

translations.text: En s'insérant sur une autoroute, la responsabilité de céder appartient à :
translations.explanation: Le véhicule qui s'insère sur l'autoroute doit céder et trouver un créneau sécuritaire dans la circulation avant d'entrer.

text: At a four-way intersection with traffic lights, if the power goes out, you should:
explanation: When traffic signals are not working, treat the intersection as an all-way stop with normal stop sign rules.

choices.text: Proceed as if you have the right of way
Treat it as a four-way stop
Only yield to traffic from the right

translations.text: En una intersección de cuatro vías con semáforos, si se corta la energía, debe:
translations.explanation: Cuando los semáforos no funcionan, trate la intersección como una parada de cuatro vías con las reglas normales de señal de alto.
choices: Proceder como si tuviera el derecho de paso
Tratarla como una parada de cuatro vías
Solo ceder el paso al tráfico de la derecha

translations.text: 在有交通灯的四路路口，如果停电，应该：
translations.explanation: 当交通信号不工作时，将路口视为全向停车标志并遵循正常停车标志规则。

translations.text: Em um cruzamento com semáforos que ficam sem energia, você deve:
translations.explanation: Quando os semáforos não funcionam, trate o cruzamento como parada obrigatória em todas as direções com regras normais de PARE.
choices: Tratar como cruzamento com quatro paradas
Apenas dar preferência ao tráfego da direita

translations.text: À une intersection à quatre voies avec feux de circulation, si l'électricité est coupée, vous devriez :
translations.explanation: Quand les feux de circulation ne fonctionnent pas, traitez l'intersection comme un arrêt à quatre voies avec les règles normales d'arrêt.
choices: Procéder comme si vous aviez la priorité
La traiter comme un arrêt à quatre voies
Céder seulement à la circulation de droite

text: Construction workers in work zones have the right of way over:
explanation: Construction workers and flaggers in designated work zones have the right of way over all vehicle traffic for safety reasons.

translations.text: Los trabajadores de construcción en zonas de trabajo tienen el derecho de paso sobre:
translations.explanation: Los trabajadores de construcción y señaleros en zonas de trabajo designadas tienen el derecho de paso sobre todo el tráfico vehicular por razones de seguridad.

translations.text: 工作区域内的建筑工人拥有优于以下车辆的路权：
translations.explanation: 指定工作区域内的建筑工人和指挥员为安全起见拥有优于所有车辆交通的路权。

translations.text: Trabalhadores da construção em zonas de obra têm preferência sobre:
translations.explanation: Trabalhadores da construção e sinalizadores em zonas de obra designadas têm preferência sobre todo o tráfego de veículos por motivos de segurança.

translations.text: Les ouvriers de la construction dans les zones de travaux ont la priorité sur :
translations.explanation: Les ouvriers de la construction et signaleurs dans les zones de travaux désignées ont la priorité sur toute circulation automobile pour des raisons de sécurité.

text: When approaching a flashing yellow light, you should:
explanation: A flashing yellow light means proceed with caution and yield the right of way to other vehicles and pedestrians as necessary.

choices.text: Proceed with caution and yield as necessary
Speed up to clear the intersection
Treat it like a stop sign

translations.text: Al aproximarse a una luz amarilla intermitente, debe:
translations.explanation: Una luz amarilla intermitente significa proceder con precaución y ceder el derecho de paso a otros vehículos y peatones según sea necesario.
choices: Proceder con precaución y ceder según sea necesario
Tratarla como una señal de alto

translations.text: 接近闪烁黄灯时，应该：
translations.explanation: 闪烁黄灯意味着谨慎通行，必要时将路权让给其他车辆和行人。
choices: 谨慎通行并必要时让行

translations.text: Ao se aproximar de uma luz amarela piscante, você deve:
translations.explanation: Uma luz amarela piscante significa prosseguir com cautela e dar preferência a outros veículos e pedestres conforme necessário.
choices: Prosseguir com cautela e dar preferência conforme necessário

translations.text: En approchant d'un feu jaune clignotant, vous devriez :
translations.explanation: Un feu jaune clignotant signifie procéder avec prudence et céder la priorité aux autres véhicules et piétons au besoin.
choices: Procéder avec prudence et céder au besoin
Le traiter comme un panneau d'arrêt

text: Mail carriers have the right of way when:
explanation: Postal vehicles making deliveries have limited right of way privileges when actively delivering mail.

translations.text: Los carteros tienen el derecho de paso cuando:
translations.explanation: Los vehículos postales haciendo entregas tienen privilegios limitados de derecho de paso cuando están entregando correo activamente.

translations.text: 邮递员拥有路权当：
translations.explanation: 邮政车辆在积极投递邮件时拥有有限的路权特权。

translations.text: Carteiros têm preferência quando:
translations.explanation: Veículos dos correios fazendo entregas têm privilégios limitados de preferência quando estão ativamente entregando correspondência.

translations.text: Les facteurs ont la priorité de passage quand :
translations.explanation: Les véhicules postaux effectuant des livraisons ont des privilèges limités de priorité quand ils livrent activement le courrier.
choices: Ils livrent le courrier depuis leur véhicule
Ils marchent vers les boîtes aux lettres

text: When exiting a parking lot, you must yield to:
explanation: When exiting a parking lot, you must yield to pedestrians on sidewalks and all traffic on the public road.

choices.text: Only vehicles traveling in your direction
Pedestrians on sidewalks and all road traffic
No one if the exit is clear

translations.text: Al salir de un estacionamiento, debe ceder el paso a:
translations.explanation: Al salir de un estacionamiento, debe ceder el paso a los peatones en las aceras y a todo el tráfico en la vía pública.
choices: Solo vehículos que viajan en su dirección
Peatones en aceras y todo el tráfico vial
Nadie si la salida está despejada

translations.text: 离开停车场时，必须让行给：
translations.explanation: 离开停车场时，必须让行给人行道上的行人和公共道路上的所有交通。
choices: 只有同方向行驶的车辆
人行道上的行人和所有道路交通
如果出口畅通则无需让行

translations.text: Ao sair de um estacionamento, você deve dar preferência a:
translations.explanation: Ao sair de um estacionamento, você deve dar preferência a pedestres nas calçadas e a todo o tráfego na via pública.
choices: Apenas veículos trafegando em sua direção
Pedestres nas calçadas e todo tráfego da via
Ninguém se a saída estiver livre

translations.text: En sortant d'un stationnement, vous devez céder le passage à :
translations.explanation: En sortant d'un stationnement, vous devez céder aux piétons sur les trottoirs et à toute circulation sur la route publique.
choices: Seulement aux véhicules dans votre direction
Les piétons sur trottoirs et toute circulation routière
Personne si la sortie est libre

text: At an intersection where you want to go straight and another vehicle wants to turn left in front of you, who has the right of way?
explanation: Straight-through traffic has the right of way over vehicles making left turns at intersections.

translations.text: En una intersección donde usted quiere ir derecho y otro vehículo quiere dar vuelta a la izquierda frente a usted, ¿quién tiene el derecho de paso?
translations.explanation: El tráfico que va derecho tiene el derecho de paso sobre los vehículos que dan vuelta a la izquierda en intersecciones.

translations.text: 在路口你想直行而另一车辆想在你面前左转时，谁拥有路权？
translations.explanation: 直行交通在路口比左转车辆拥有路权。

translations.text: Em um cruzamento onde você quer seguir em frente e outro veículo quer converter à esquerda na sua frente, quem tem a preferência?
translations.explanation: Tráfego seguindo em frente tem preferência sobre veículos fazendo conversões à esquerda em cruzamentos.

translations.text: À une intersection où vous voulez aller tout droit et un autre véhicule veut tourner à gauche devant vous, qui a la priorité ?
translations.explanation: La circulation allant tout droit a la priorité sur les véhicules effectuant des virages à gauche aux intersections.

text: You arrive at a four-way stop intersection at the same time as another vehicle to your right. Both vehicles are going straight. Who has the right of way?
explanation: When two vehicles arrive simultaneously at a four-way stop, the vehicle on the right has the right of way.

choices.text: You have the right of way
The vehicle to your right has the right of way
The larger vehicle has the right of way
Whoever honks first has the right of way

translations.text: 您和右侧的另一辆车同时到达四向停车路口。两车都直行。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 当两辆车同时到达四向停车路口时，右侧车辆有先行权。
choices: 您右侧的车辆有先行权

translations.text: Você chega a um cruzamento com parada obrigatória em quatro direções ao mesmo tempo que outro veículo à sua direita. Ambos os veículos seguem em frente. Quem tem a preferência?
translations.explanation: Quando dois veículos chegam simultaneamente a uma parada obrigatória em quatro direções, o veículo à direita tem a preferência.
choices: O veículo à sua direita tem a preferência
O veículo maior tem a preferência
Quem buzinar primeiro tem a preferência

text: You're turning left at an intersection with a green light. An oncoming vehicle is going straight through the same intersection. What should you do?
explanation: Left-turning vehicles must yield to oncoming traffic going straight, even when both have a green light.

choices.text: Flash your headlights to claim right of way
Turn quickly before the oncoming vehicle reaches the intersection

translations.text: 您在绿灯路口左转。对面有车辆直行通过同一路口。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 左转车辆必须让行对面直行车辆，即使双方都是绿灯。
choices: 在对面车辆到达路口前快速转弯

translations.text: Você está fazendo uma conversão à esquerda em um cruzamento com sinal verde. Um veículo na direção oposta está seguindo em frente pelo mesmo cruzamento. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Veículos fazendo conversão à esquerda devem dar preferência ao tráfego que vem em sentido contrário seguindo em frente, mesmo quando ambos têm sinal verde.
choices: Dar preferência ao veículo que vem em sentido contrário
Piscar os faróis para reivindicar a preferência
Converter rapidamente antes que o veículo que vem em sentido contrário chegue ao cruzamento

text: You're driving on a residential street and encounter a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop sign. The bus is on the opposite side of a divided highway with a median barrier. What must you do?
explanation: When a physical barrier separates you from a school bus with flashing lights, you are not required to stop.

choices.text: Stop until the lights stop flashing
Stop only if children are visible
Continue at normal speed since you're separated by a median

translations.text: 您在住宅街道行驶时遇到校车闪红灯并伸出停车标志。校车在有中央隔离带的分离式道路对面。您必须怎么做?
translations.explanation: 当物理隔离带将您与闪灯校车分开时，您无需停车。
choices: 只有看到儿童时才停车
由于有中央隔离带分隔可正常速度通行

translations.text: Você está dirigindo em uma rua residencial e encontra um ônibus escolar com luzes vermelhas piscando e placa de pare estendida. O ônibus está do lado oposto de uma rodovia dividida com barreira central. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Quando uma barreira física o separa de um ônibus escolar com luzes piscando, você não é obrigado a parar.
choices: Parar até que as luzes parem de piscar
Diminuir a velocidade mas continuar dirigindo
Parar apenas se crianças estiverem visíveis
Continuar em velocidade normal já que está separado pela barreira central

text: At an intersection controlled by a traffic light, the power goes out and the light is completely dark. How should you treat this intersection?
explanation: When traffic signals are not functioning, the intersection should be treated as an all-way stop.

choices.text: Treat it as a four-way stop
The main road has right of way

translations.text: 在交通信号灯控制的路口，停电导致信号灯完全熄灭。您应该如何对待这个路口?
translations.explanation: 当交通信号灯失效时，应将路口当作全向停车标志处理。

translations.text: Em um cruzamento controlado por semáforo, a energia acaba e o sinal está completamente apagado. Como você deve tratar este cruzamento?
translations.explanation: Quando os semáforos não estão funcionando, o cruzamento deve ser tratado como uma parada obrigatória em todas as direções.
choices: Tratar como parada obrigatória em quatro direções
Primeiro a chegar, primeiro a passar

text: You're merging onto a highway from an on-ramp. Traffic on the highway is heavy. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Vehicles already on the highway have the right of way over merging traffic. Merging vehicles must find a safe gap to enter.

choices.text: You have the right of way as the merging vehicle
Highway traffic has the right of way
Whoever is traveling faster has the right of way
The right of way alternates between merging and highway traffic

translations.text: 您从匝道汇入高速公路。高速公路交通繁忙。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 高速公路上的车辆对汇入车辆有先行权。汇入车辆必须寻找安全空隙进入。
choices: 您作为汇入车辆有先行权
高速公路车辆有先行权
速度快的车辆有先行权
汇入车辆和高速公路车辆轮流享有先行权

translations.text: Você está entrando em uma rodovia através de uma alça de acesso. O tráfego na rodovia está intenso. Quem tem a preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos que já estão na rodovia têm preferência sobre o tráfego que está entrando. Veículos entrando devem encontrar uma brecha segura para entrar.
choices: Você tem preferência como veículo entrando
O tráfego da rodovia tem preferência
Quem estiver viajando mais rápido tem preferência
A preferência alterna entre tráfego entrando e da rodovia

text: You're at a T-intersection where your road ends and you must turn either left or right onto another road. A vehicle approaches from your left wanting to turn left onto your road. Who yields?
explanation: Vehicles entering a through street from a terminating road must yield to all traffic on the through street.

choices.text: You yield to the approaching vehicle
The approaching vehicle yields to you
Whoever arrives first has right of way
The vehicle making the sharper turn yields

translations.text: 您在丁字路口，您的道路终止，必须左转或右转到另一条路上。一辆车从您左侧驶来要左转进入您的道路。谁让行?
translations.explanation: 从终止道路进入主干道的车辆必须让行主干道上的所有车辆。
choices: 转弯幅度大的车辆让行

translations.text: Você está em um cruzamento em T onde sua via termina e você deve dobrar à esquerda ou à direita em outra via. Um veículo se aproxima pela sua esquerda querendo dobrar à esquerda na sua via. Quem dá preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos entrando em uma via preferencial a partir de uma via que termina devem dar preferência a todo o tráfego da via preferencial.
choices: Você dá preferência ao veículo que se aproxima
O veículo que se aproxima dá preferência a você
O veículo fazendo a curva mais fechada dá preferência

text: An emergency vehicle with flashing lights and sirens is approaching from behind while you're in the left lane of a four-lane highway. What should you do?
explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by moving to the right and slowing down or stopping when safe to do so.

choices.text: Stop immediately in your current lane
Speed up to get out of the way
Move to the right lane when safe and slow down
Continue driving normally since you're not blocking them

translations.text: 一辆闪灯鸣笛的紧急车辆从后方驶来，您在四车道高速公路的左车道。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 您必须在安全时向右移动并减速或停车来让行紧急车辆。
choices: 安全时移到右车道并减速
正常行驶因为没有阻挡它们

translations.text: Um veículo de emergência com luzes piscando e sirenes está se aproximando por trás enquanto você está na faixa da esquerda de uma rodovia de quatro faixas. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Você deve dar preferência a veículos de emergência movendo-se para a direita e diminuindo a velocidade ou parando quando seguro fazê-lo.
choices: Parar imediatamente na sua faixa atual
Mover-se para a faixa da direita quando seguro e diminuir a velocidade
Continuar dirigindo normalmente já que não está bloqueando-os

text: You're turning right on a red light after coming to a complete stop. A pedestrian enters the crosswalk you're about to cross. What is required?
explanation: Pedestrians in crosswalks always have the right of way, even when vehicles are making legal turns on red.

choices.text: Proceed since you have vehicular right of way
Proceed slowly while the pedestrian moves faster

translations.text: 您完全停车后红灯右转。一名行人进入您即将通过的人行横道。要求是什么?
translations.explanation: 人行横道上的行人总是有先行权，即使车辆合法红灯右转。
choices: 继续行驶因为您有车辆先行权
缓慢前进同时行人加快通过

translations.text: Você está dobrando à direita no sinal vermelho após parar completamente. Um pedestre entra na faixa de pedestres que você está prestes a atravessar. O que é necessário?
translations.explanation: Pedestres em faixas de pedestres sempre têm preferência, mesmo quando veículos estão fazendo conversões legais no vermelho.
choices: Prosseguir já que você tem preferência veicular
Prosseguir devagar enquanto o pedestre se move mais rápido

text: You approach an intersection where a police officer is directing traffic with hand signals, but the traffic light shows green for your direction. What should you do?
explanation: A police officer's hand signals override all traffic control devices including traffic lights and signs.

translations.text: 您接近路口时警察在用手势指挥交通，但您方向的交通灯显示绿灯。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 警察的手势指挥优先于所有交通控制设备，包括交通灯和标志。

translations.text: Você se aproxima de um cruzamento onde um policial está direcionando o tráfego com sinais manuais, mas o semáforo mostra verde para sua direção. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Os sinais manuais de um policial têm precedência sobre todos os dispositivos de controle de tráfego, incluindo semáforos e placas.

text: Two vehicles approach a narrow bridge at the same time from opposite directions. The bridge can only accommodate one vehicle. Who has the right of way?
explanation: On narrow bridges or mountain roads, the first vehicle to arrive has the right of way to proceed.

choices.text: The first vehicle to reach the bridge

translations.text: 两辆车从相反方向同时接近只能通过一辆车的狭窄桥梁。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 在狭窄桥梁或山路上，先到达的车辆有先行权。

translations.text: Dois veículos se aproximam de uma ponte estreita ao mesmo tempo de direções opostas. A ponte só pode acomodar um veículo. Quem tem preferência?
translations.explanation: Em pontes estreitas ou estradas de montanha, o primeiro veículo a chegar tem preferência para prosseguir.
choices: O primeiro veículo a chegar à ponte

text: You're in a parking lot and approaching the exit to enter a public street. A vehicle on the street wants to turn into the parking lot. Who yields?
explanation: Vehicles exiting private property (like parking lots) must yield to all traffic on the public roadway.

choices.text: You yield to the vehicle on the street
The vehicle on the street yields to you
The larger vehicle has right of way

translations.text: 您在停车场接近出口准备进入公共街道。街道上有车辆要转入停车场。谁让行?
translations.explanation: 从私人场地（如停车场）驶出的车辆必须让行公共道路上的所有车辆。

translations.text: Você está em um estacionamento se aproximando da saída para entrar em uma via pública. Um veículo na rua quer entrar no estacionamento. Quem dá preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos saindo de propriedade privada (como estacionamentos) devem dar preferência a todo o tráfego da via pública.
choices: Você dá preferência ao veículo na rua
O veículo na rua dá preferência a você
Quem estiver dobrando à esquerda dá preferência

text: At a roundabout, you want to enter while another vehicle is already circulating inside. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Vehicles already in the roundabout have the right of way over vehicles waiting to enter.

choices.text: You have the right of way as entering traffic
The circulating vehicle has the right of way
Whoever is larger has the right of way
Right of way alternates between entering and circulating traffic

translations.text: 在环形路口，您想进入时另一辆车已在环内行驶。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 已在环形路口内行驶的车辆对等待进入的车辆有先行权。
choices: 您作为进入车辆有先行权
环内行驶车辆有先行权
进入和环行车辆轮流享有先行权

translations.text: Em uma rotatória, você quer entrar enquanto outro veículo já está circulando dentro. Quem tem preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos já na rotatória têm preferência sobre veículos esperando para entrar.
choices: Você tem preferência como tráfego entrando
A preferência alterna entre tráfego entrando e circulando

text: You're driving straight through an intersection when a vehicle runs a red light from your left. You have a green light. Who is legally at fault if an accident occurs?
explanation: The driver who runs the red light is at fault, as vehicles with green lights have the right of way.

choices.text: You are at fault for not avoiding the accident
The other driver is at fault for running the red light
The fault depends on who entered the intersection first

translations.text: 您直行通过路口时，左侧有车闯红灯。您是绿灯。如发生事故谁在法律上有过错?
translations.explanation: 闯红灯的司机有过错，因为绿灯车辆有先行权。
choices: 您有过错因为没有避免事故
对方司机有过错因为闯红灯
双方司机平等承担过错
过错取决于谁先进入路口

translations.text: Você está dirigindo em linha reta através de um cruzamento quando um veículo avança o sinal vermelho pela sua esquerda. Você tem sinal verde. Quem é legalmente culpado se ocorrer um acidente?
translations.explanation: O motorista que avança o sinal vermelho é culpado, pois veículos com sinal verde têm preferência.
choices: Você é culpado por não evitar o acidente
O outro motorista é culpado por avançar o sinal vermelho
Ambos os motoristas compartilham culpa igual
A culpa depende de quem entrou primeiro no cruzamento

text: A pedestrian is jaywalking across the street outside of a crosswalk. You have the right of way, but what is your legal obligation?
explanation: Even when pedestrians are jaywalking, drivers have a duty to avoid accidents when reasonably possible.

choices.text: You may proceed at normal speed
You must still avoid hitting the pedestrian if possible
The pedestrian must get out of your way immediately

translations.text: 行人在人行横道外乱穿马路。您有先行权，但您的法律义务是什么?
translations.explanation: 即使行人乱穿马路，司机仍有义务在合理可能的情况下避免事故。
choices: 如果可能您仍必须避免撞到行人

translations.text: Um pedestre está atravessando a rua irregularmente fora da faixa de pedestres. Você tem preferência, mas qual é sua obrigação legal?
translations.explanation: Mesmo quando pedestres estão atravessando irregularmente, motoristas têm o dever de evitar acidentes quando razoavelmente possível.
choices: Você pode prosseguir em velocidade normal
Você ainda deve evitar atropelar o pedestre se possível
O pedestre deve sair do seu caminho imediatamente

text: You're making a U-turn at an intersection. An oncoming vehicle is turning right. Both moves are legal. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Vehicles making U-turns must yield to all other traffic, including vehicles turning right from the opposite direction.

choices.text: You have the right of way making the U-turn
The vehicle turning right has the right of way

translations.text: 您在路口掉头。对面车辆右转。两个动作都合法。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 掉头车辆必须让行所有其他车辆，包括对面右转的车辆。

translations.text: Você está fazendo retorno em um cruzamento. Um veículo em sentido contrário está dobrando à direita. Ambas as manobras são legais. Quem tem preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos fazendo retorno devem dar preferência a todo o outro tráfego, incluindo veículos dobrando à direita da direção oposta.
choices: Você tem preferência fazendo o retorno
O veículo dobrando à direita tem preferência

text: At a four-way stop, you arrive first and stop. As you begin to proceed, another vehicle arrives and stops. What should happen?
explanation: The first vehicle to arrive and stop at a four-way stop has the right of way to proceed first.

choices.text: The other vehicle should wait for you to clear the intersection
You should let the other vehicle go first to be courteous
Whoever is making the right turn goes first
The vehicle on the right has automatic priority

translations.text: 在四向停车路口，您先到达并停车。当您开始通行时，另一辆车到达并停车。应该怎样?
translations.explanation: 在四向停车路口先到达并停车的车辆有先行权优先通行。
choices: 其他车辆应等您通过路口
您应该礼貌地让其他车辆先行

translations.text: Em uma parada obrigatória em quatro direções, você chega primeiro e para. Quando você começa a prosseguir, outro veículo chega e para. O que deve acontecer?
translations.explanation: O primeiro veículo a chegar e parar em uma parada obrigatória em quatro direções tem preferência para prosseguir primeiro.
choices: O outro veículo deve esperar você liberar o cruzamento
Você deve deixar o outro veículo passar primeiro por cortesia
Quem estiver dobrando à direita vai primeiro
O veículo à direita tem prioridade automática

text: You're turning left from a two-way street onto a one-way street. Pedestrians are crossing the one-way street from both sides. Who do you yield to?
explanation: When turning, drivers must yield to all pedestrians crossing the street they are entering, regardless of direction.

choices.text: Only pedestrians crossing from the right
Only pedestrians crossing from the left
No one, since you have the turning right of way

translations.text: 您从双向街道左转进入单行道。行人从两侧穿越单行道。您让行谁?
translations.explanation: 转弯时，司机必须让行所有穿越其要进入街道的行人，无论方向如何。
choices: 只让行从右侧穿越的行人
只让行从左侧穿越的行人
让行所有穿越街道的行人
无需让行，因为您有转弯先行权

translations.text: Você está dobrando à esquerda de uma via de mão dupla para uma via de mão única. Pedestres estão atravessando a via de mão única dos dois lados. A quem você dá preferência?
translations.explanation: Ao fazer conversões, motoristas devem dar preferência a todos os pedestres atravessando a rua que estão entrando, independente da direção.
choices: Todos os pedestres atravessando a rua
Ninguém, já que você tem preferência de conversão

text: A funeral procession with headlights on is traveling through an intersection. You arrive at the intersection with a green light. What should you do?
explanation: Funeral processions have the right of way and should not be interrupted, even by vehicles with green lights.

choices.text: Proceed since you have a green light
Yield to the entire funeral procession
Only yield to the lead vehicle
Proceed but honk to warn the procession

translations.text: 开着前灯的送葬队伍通过路口。您到达路口时是绿灯。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 送葬队伍有先行权，不应被打断，即使其他车辆是绿灯。
choices: 继续行驶因为您是绿灯

translations.text: Um cortejo fúnebre com faróis acesos está passando por um cruzamento. Você chega ao cruzamento com sinal verde. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Cortejos fúnebres têm preferência e não devem ser interrompidos, mesmo por veículos com sinal verde.
choices: Prosseguir já que você tem sinal verde
Dar preferência a todo o cortejo fúnebre
Dar preferência apenas ao veículo da frente
Prosseguir mas buzinar para avisar o cortejo

text: You're in the right lane approaching an intersection. A vehicle in the left lane signals to turn right across your lane. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Vehicles cannot turn across other lanes of traffic without yielding; you have the right of way to continue in your lane.

choices.text: The signaling vehicle because they signaled first
You have the right of way in your lane

translations.text: 您在右车道接近路口。左车道的车辆示意要右转穿越您的车道。谁有先行权?
translations.explanation: 车辆不能在不让行的情况下跨越其他车道转弯；您有在自己车道内继续行驶的先行权。
choices: 您在自己车道有先行权

translations.text: Você está na faixa da direita se aproximando de um cruzamento. Um veículo na faixa da esquerda sinaliza para dobrar à direita atravessando sua faixa. Quem tem preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos não podem dobrar através de outras faixas de tráfego sem dar preferência; você tem preferência para continuar em sua faixa.
choices: O veículo sinalizando porque sinalizou primeiro
Você tem preferência em sua faixa

text: You're backing out of a driveway onto a residential street. A child on a bicycle is riding on the sidewalk approaching your driveway. Who yields?
explanation: Vehicles backing from driveways must yield to all pedestrians, cyclists, and traffic on the sidewalk and street.

choices.text: The child must stop for your vehicle
You must yield to the child
The child should ride around your vehicle

translations.text: 您从车道倒车驶入住宅街道。一名儿童骑自行车在人行道上接近您的车道。谁让行?
translations.explanation: 从车道倒车的车辆必须让行人行道和街道上的所有行人、骑车者和车辆。
choices: 儿童必须为您的车辆停车
儿童应该绕过您的车辆

translations.text: Você está saindo de ré de uma garagem para uma rua residencial. Uma criança em uma bicicleta está andando na calçada se aproximando da sua garagem. Quem dá preferência?
translations.explanation: Veículos saindo de ré de garagens devem dar preferência a todos os pedestres, ciclistas e tráfego na calçada e rua.
choices: A criança deve parar para seu veículo
Você deve dar preferência à criança
A criança deve contornar seu veículo

text: At an intersection, you have a flashing yellow light while cross traffic has a flashing red light. How do you proceed?
explanation: Flashing yellow means proceed with caution while maintaining right of way over traffic with flashing red lights.

choices.text: Stop and wait for cross traffic
Proceed with caution but maintain right of way
Treat it as a four-way stop

translations.text: 在路口，您看到黄色闪灯而横向车流是红色闪灯。您如何通行?
translations.explanation: 黄色闪灯意味着谨慎通行但对红色闪灯车辆保持先行权。
choices: 谨慎通行但保持先行权

translations.text: Em um cruzamento, você tem uma luz amarela piscante enquanto o tráfego cruzado tem uma luz vermelha piscante. Como você prossegue?
translations.explanation: Luz amarela piscante significa prosseguir com cautela mantendo preferência sobre tráfego com luzes vermelhas piscantes.
choices: Parar e esperar pelo tráfego cruzado
Prosseguir com cautela mas manter preferência
Tratar como parada obrigatória em quatro direções

text: You're driving on a main road and a vehicle from a side street is waiting to turn left across your path. They begin to turn as you approach. What describes the right of way?
explanation: Through traffic on the main road has right of way over vehicles turning left from side streets.

choices.text: They have right of way once they start turning
You maintain right of way as through traffic
Right of way goes to whoever reaches the intersection first
You must slow down to let them complete the turn

translations.text: 您在主路行驶，支路有车等待左转穿越您的路径。当您接近时它们开始转弯。先行权如何?
translations.explanation: 主路上的直行车辆对从支路左转的车辆有先行权。
choices: 它们一旦开始转弯就有先行权
您作为直行车辆保持先行权
先到达路口者有先行权
您必须减速让它们完成转弯

translations.text: Você está dirigindo em uma via principal e um veículo de uma rua lateral está esperando para dobrar à esquerda atravessando seu caminho. Eles começam a dobrar quando você se aproxima. O que descreve a preferência?
translations.explanation: Tráfego direto na via principal tem preferência sobre veículos dobrando à esquerda de ruas laterais.
choices: Eles têm preferência uma vez que começam a dobrar
Você mantém preferência como tráfego direto
Preferência vai para quem chegar primeiro ao cruzamento
Você deve diminuir a velocidade para deixá-los completar a conversão

text: A mail truck is stopped in the right lane with hazard lights flashing while making deliveries. You're approaching from behind. What should you do?
explanation: You should safely change lanes to pass stopped service vehicles with hazard lights when it's legal and safe to do so.

choices.text: Wait behind the mail truck until it moves
Carefully change lanes to pass when safe
Honk to make the truck move

translations.text: 邮车在右车道停车，闪着危险警示灯送邮件。您从后方接近。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 在合法且安全的情况下，您应该安全地变道超越闪着危险警示灯的停车服务车辆。
choices: 在邮车后等待直到它移动

translations.text: Um caminhão dos correios está parado na faixa da direita com luzes de emergência piscando fazendo entregas. Você está se aproximando por trás. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Você deve com segurança mudar de faixa para ultrapassar veículos de serviço parados com luzes de emergência quando for legal e seguro fazê-lo.
choices: Esperar atrás do caminhão dos correios até ele se mover
Cuidadosamente mudar de faixa para ultrapassar quando seguro
Buzinar para fazer o caminhão se mover

text: You arrive at a stop sign at the same time as another vehicle directly across from you. You want to turn left, they want to go straight. Who proceeds first?
explanation: When vehicles arrive simultaneously at a stop sign, straight-through traffic has right of way over turning traffic.

choices.text: You proceed first since you're turning
They proceed first since they're going straight

translations.text: 您和正对面的另一辆车同时到达停车标志。您要左转，它们要直行。谁先通行?
translations.explanation: 当车辆同时到达停车标志时，直行车辆对转弯车辆有先行权。
choices: 它们先行因为它们直行

translations.text: Você chega a uma placa de pare ao mesmo tempo que outro veículo diretamente à sua frente. Você quer dobrar à esquerda, eles querem seguir em frente. Quem prossegue primeiro?
translations.explanation: Quando veículos chegam simultaneamente a uma placa de pare, tráfego seguindo em frente tem preferência sobre tráfego fazendo conversão.
choices: Você prossegue primeiro já que está dobrando
Eles prosseguem primeiro já que estão seguindo em frente

text: An ambulance with lights and sirens is approaching an intersection from your right. You have a green light to go straight. What should you do?
explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles before entering an intersection, even when you have a green light.

choices.text: Enter the intersection then pull over
Continue but yield inside the intersection

translations.text: 救护车闪着灯鸣着笛从您右侧接近路口。您有绿灯直行。您应该怎么办?
translations.explanation: 即使您有绿灯，在进入路口前也必须让行紧急车辆。
choices: 继续行驶但在路口内让行

translations.text: Uma ambulância com luzes e sirenes está se aproximando de um cruzamento pela sua direita. Você tem sinal verde para seguir em frente. O que você deve fazer?
translations.explanation: Você deve dar preferência a veículos de emergência antes de entrar no cruzamento, mesmo quando você tem sinal verde.
choices: Parar antes de entrar no cruzamento
Continuar mas dar preferência dentro do cruzamento

text: You arrive at a four-way intersection with stop signs at the same time as another vehicle directly across from you. You want to turn left, and they want to go straight. Who has the right of way?
explanation: When two vehicles arrive simultaneously at opposite sides of an intersection, straight-through traffic has the right of way over turning traffic.

choices.text: You have the right of way because you stopped first
The other vehicle has the right of way
Whoever honks first gets the right of way
You must both wait for traffic from the right

translations.text: 您和另一辆车同时到达一个四向停车标志的交叉路口，对方车辆在您正对面。您想左转，对方想直行。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 当两辆车同时到达交叉路口的对面时，直行车辆比转弯车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 您有优先通行权，因为您先停车
对方车辆有优先通行权
你们都必须等待右侧来车

text: You're driving on a residential street and approach a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop sign. The bus is on the opposite side of a divided highway with a concrete median barrier. What should you do?
explanation: When a physical barrier separates you from a school bus, you are not required to stop, as children cannot cross from the bus to your side of traffic.

choices.text: Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing
Slow down to 15 mph and proceed with caution
Continue at normal speed since you're separated by a barrier
Stop only if children are visible

translations.text: 您在住宅街道行驶，遇到一辆闪着红灯并伸出停车标志的校车。该校车在分隔公路的对面，中间有混凝土隔离带。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 当物理隔离带将您与校车分开时，您不需要停车，因为儿童无法从校车穿越到您这一侧的车道。
choices: 停车等待直到红灯停止闪烁
减速至15英里/小时并谨慎通过
正常速度继续行驶，因为被隔离带分开
只有看到儿童时才停车

text: What is the maximum speed limit typically posted on most highways?
explanation: Highway speed limits are typically posted between 65-70 mph in most states, designed for safe high-speed travel.

translations.text: ¿Cuál es el límite de velocidad máximo que se publica típicamente en la mayoría de las autopistas?
translations.explanation: Los límites de velocidad en autopistas típicamente se publican entre 65-70 mph en la mayoría de los estados, diseñados para viajes seguros a alta velocidad.

translations.text: 大多数高速公路上通常标示的最高限速是多少？
translations.explanation: 高速公路限速通常在大多数州标示为每小时65-70英里，旨在保证安全的高速行驶。

translations.text: Qual é o limite máximo de velocidade normalmente sinalizado na maioria das rodovias?
translations.explanation: Os limites de velocidade em rodovias são tipicamente sinalizados entre 105-110 km/h na maioria dos estados, projetados para viagens seguras em alta velocidade.

translations.text: Quelle est la vitesse maximale généralement affichée sur la plupart des autoroutes?
translations.explanation: Les limitations de vitesse sur autoroute sont généralement affichées entre 105-115 km/h dans la plupart des États, conçues pour une circulation sécurisée à haute vitesse.

text: At an intersection with a malfunctioning traffic light, you arrive second behind another car that's already stopped. How do you proceed?
explanation: At malfunctioning traffic lights, treat the intersection as a four-way stop where vehicles proceed in the order they arrived.

choices.text: Follow the first car through immediately
Wait for the first car to go, then treat it as your turn at a four-way stop
Honk to signal your intention to proceed
Go around the first car if they hesitate

translations.text: 在交通信号灯故障的交叉路口，您排在另一辆已停车的车后面。您应该如何通行？
translations.explanation: 在交通信号灯故障时，将交叉路口视为四向停车路口，车辆按到达顺序通行。
choices: 等前车通过后，按四向停车路口规则轮到您
如果前车犹豫就绕过去

text: You're merging onto a highway from an on-ramp. Traffic on the highway is heavy but moving. Who is responsible for ensuring a safe merge?
explanation: Merging traffic must yield to traffic already on the highway and find a safe gap to enter.

choices.text: Highway traffic must slow down to let you in
You must find a safe gap and adjust your speed accordingly
Whoever is driving the larger vehicle has priority
You have the right of way since you're accelerating

translations.text: 您从匝道汇入高速公路。高速公路上交通繁忙但在移动。谁负责确保安全汇入？
translations.explanation: 汇入车辆必须让行给高速公路上的车辆，并找到安全间隙进入。
choices: 高速公路上的车辆必须减速让您汇入
您必须找到安全间隙并相应调整车速
车辆较大的一方有优先权
您有优先通行权因为您在加速

text: While driving straight through an intersection on a green light, a pedestrian suddenly enters the crosswalk against the signal. What should you do?
explanation: Even when pedestrians are crossing illegally, drivers must yield to avoid accidents, as pedestrians are vulnerable road users.

choices.text: Continue through since you have the green light
Stop and yield to the pedestrian
Honk and flash your lights to warn the pedestrian
Speed up to clear the intersection quickly

translations.text: 您在绿灯时直行通过交叉路口，一名行人突然违反信号进入人行横道。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 即使行人违法过马路，驾驶员也必须让行以避免事故，因为行人是脆弱的道路使用者。
choices: 继续通过因为您是绿灯
鸣喇叭并闪灯警告行人
加速快速通过交叉路口

text: You're at a T-intersection where you're on the road that ends. There's no stop sign, but you need to turn either left or right onto the through road. Who has the right of way?
explanation: At T-intersections, traffic on the continuing through road always has the right of way over traffic that must turn from the terminating road.

choices.text: You have the right of way when turning right only
Traffic on the through road has the right of way
Whoever arrives first has the right of way
You have equal right of way in both directions

translations.text: 您在T形交叉路口，您所在的道路到此结束。没有停车标志，但您需要向左或向右转入直行道路。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 在T形交叉路口，直行道路上的车辆总是比必须从终止道路转弯的车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 您只有右转时才有优先通行权
直行道路上的车辆有优先通行权
先到达者有优先通行权
您在两个方向都有同等优先通行权

text: You're in the left turn lane with a green arrow. As you begin turning, the arrow turns yellow then red, but you're already in the intersection. What should you do?
explanation: If you're already in the intersection when the light changes, you should complete your turn safely rather than stop in the intersection.

choices.text: Back up to the stop line
Wait in the intersection for the next green arrow

translations.text: 您在左转车道等绿色箭头。当您开始转弯时，箭头变成黄色然后红色，但您已经在交叉路口内。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 如果您在信号灯变化时已经在交叉路口内，应该安全完成转弯而不是在交叉路口内停车。
choices: 立即在交叉路口内停车
在交叉路口内等待下一个绿色箭头

text: An emergency vehicle with sirens and flashing lights is approaching from behind while you're in heavy traffic on a multi-lane road. What's the proper response?
explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by moving to the right and stopping when you can do so safely, not by stopping immediately in traffic.

choices.text: Move to the right and stop when it's safe to do so
Speed up to get out of the way
Flash your hazard lights and continue driving

translations.text: 一辆带警报器和闪光灯的紧急车辆从后方驶来，您正在多车道道路的拥挤交通中行驶。正确的应对方式是什么？
translations.explanation: 您必须在安全的情况下向右移动并停车来为紧急车辆让行，而不是在交通中立即停车。
choices: 立即在您的车道内停车
在安全的情况下向右移动并停车
闪危险警示灯并继续行驶

text: At a roundabout, you're approaching the entrance while a cyclist is already circulating inside. Who has the right of way?
explanation: In roundabouts, all circulating traffic (including bicycles) has the right of way over entering traffic.

choices.text: You have the right of way because cars are faster
The cyclist must yield to entering vehicles
The cyclist has the right of way as circulating traffic
Whoever is larger has the right of way

translations.text: 在环形交叉路口，您正在接近入口，一名骑自行车者已经在环形路内行驶。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 在环形交叉路口，所有环形路内行驶的车辆（包括自行车）比进入的车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 您有优先通行权因为汽车更快
骑自行车者必须让行给进入的车辆
作为环形路内行驶车辆，骑自行车者有优先通行权
体积较大者有优先通行权

text: You're turning right on red after coming to a complete stop. A pedestrian starts crossing the street you're turning onto just as you begin your turn. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Pedestrians in crosswalks always have the right of way, even when vehicles are making legal turns on red.

choices.text: You have the right of way since you stopped first
The pedestrian has the right of way
Whoever reaches the crosswalk first has priority
You have the right of way only if the pedestrian is jaywalking

translations.text: 您在完全停车后红灯右转。正当您开始转弯时，一名行人开始穿越您要转入的街道。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 人行横道上的行人总是有优先通行权，即使车辆在合法的红灯右转。
choices: 您有优先通行权因为您先停车
谁先到达人行横道谁优先
只有当行人乱穿马路时您才有优先通行权

text: Two vehicles approach a narrow bridge that can only accommodate one vehicle at a time. One vehicle is clearly closer to the bridge. How is right of way determined?
explanation: At narrow bridges or passages, the vehicle that reaches the area first has the right of way to proceed.

choices.text: The larger vehicle has the right of way
The vehicle that can cross faster goes first
The vehicle closer to the bridge has the right of way
Both vehicles must back up and try again

translations.text: 两辆车接近一座只能容纳一辆车通过的狭窄桥梁。其中一辆车明显更靠近桥梁。优先通行权如何确定？
translations.explanation: 在狭窄的桥梁或通道，先到达该区域的车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 较大的车辆有优先通行权
能更快通过的车辆先行
更靠近桥梁的车辆有优先通行权
两辆车都必须后退重新尝试

text: You're driving on a main road and a vehicle from a private driveway is trying to enter traffic. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Traffic on public roads always has the right of way over vehicles entering from private driveways or parking lots.

choices.text: The vehicle entering has the right of way if they signal
You have the right of way on the main road
Whoever is driving more slowly has the right of way
The right of way alternates every 30 seconds

translations.text: 您在主路上行驶，一辆车从私人车道试图驶入交通流。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 公共道路上的车辆总是比从私人车道或停车场驶入的车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 如果驶入车辆打信号灯就有优先通行权
您在主路上有优先通行权
开得较慢的一方有优先通行权
优先通行权每30秒轮换一次

text: At an intersection, you have a flashing yellow light while cross traffic has a flashing red light. How do you proceed?
explanation: Flashing yellow means proceed with caution after yielding to other traffic, while flashing red means others must stop and yield to you.

choices.text: Stop and wait for the flashing to end
Proceed with caution after yielding to cross traffic
Stop and treat it like a four-way stop
Proceed normally as if you have a green light

translations.text: 在交叉路口，您遇到闪烁黄灯，而交叉车流是闪烁红灯。您应该如何通行？
translations.explanation: 闪烁黄灯表示在让行其他车辆后谨慎通行，而闪烁红灯表示其他人必须停车让行给您。
choices: 在让行交叉车流后谨慎通行

text: You're making a left turn at an intersection and an oncoming motorcycle is approaching. The motorcycle appears to be farther away than a car would need to be for you to turn safely. What should you consider?
explanation: Motorcycles can be difficult to see and judge for distance and speed, so extra caution is needed when determining if it's safe to turn.

choices.text: Motorcycles must yield to turning cars
Motorcycles are easier to stop quickly
Motorcycles can be difficult to judge for speed and distance
Motorcycles always have the right of way

translations.text: 您在交叉路口左转，一辆迎面而来的摩托车正在接近。摩托车看起来比汽车需要安全转弯的距离更远。您应该考虑什么？
translations.explanation: 摩托车可能难以看清并判断距离和速度，因此在判断是否安全转弯时需要格外谨慎。
choices: 摩托车必须让行给转弯的汽车
摩托车更容易快速停车
摩托车的速度和距离难以判断
摩托车总是有优先通行权

text: You're at a stop sign and want to turn right. A cyclist is approaching from your left, traveling straight on the road you want to enter. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Cyclists on the through road have the same right of way as motor vehicles, and turning traffic must yield to through traffic.

choices.text: You have the right of way after stopping
The cyclist has the right of way as through traffic
Whoever is heavier has the right of way
You can proceed if you signal properly

translations.text: 您在停车标志处想右转。一名骑自行车者从您左侧接近，在您要进入的道路上直行。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 直行道路上的骑自行车者与机动车有同等优先通行权，转弯车辆必须让行给直行车辆。
choices: 您停车后有优先通行权
作为直行车辆，骑自行车者有优先通行权
较重的一方有优先通行权
如果您正确打信号就可以通行

text: While driving, you encounter a funeral procession that has not yet completely passed through an intersection. What should you do?
explanation: Funeral processions have the right of way to stay together, and other traffic should not break up the procession by driving through it.

choices.text: Join the end of the procession
Wait until the entire procession passes
Drive through if you have a green light
Honk to alert them of your presence

translations.text: 行驶中，您遇到尚未完全通过交叉路口的送葬队伍。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 送葬队伍有保持队形的优先通行权，其他车辆不应该穿过队伍将其分散。
choices: 如果您是绿灯就可以穿过

text: You're in a parking lot and approaching the exit onto a public street. A pedestrian is walking along the sidewalk on the public street. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Vehicles exiting parking lots must yield to pedestrians on public sidewalks and walkways.

choices.text: You have the right of way since you're in a vehicle
The pedestrian has the right of way
Whoever reaches the area first has priority
You have equal right of way

translations.text: 您在停车场内接近出口进入公共街道。一名行人正在公共街道的人行道上行走。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 从停车场驶出的车辆必须让行给公共人行道和步道上的行人。
choices: 您有优先通行权因为您开车
谁先到达该区域谁优先
你们有同等优先通行权

text: At a four-way intersection with traffic lights, your light is green but there's a police officer directing traffic with hand signals that contradict the light. What should you do?
explanation: When present, a police officer's hand signals always take precedence over traffic lights or signs.

choices.text: Follow the traffic light since it's automated
Stop and ask the officer which to follow
Follow whichever gives you the right of way

translations.text: 在有交通信号灯的四向交叉路口，您的灯是绿色但有警察在用手势指挥交通，手势与信号灯矛盾。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 有警察在场时，警察的手势指挥总是优先于交通信号灯或标志。
choices: 遵循交通信号灯因为它是自动的
停车询问警察该遵循哪个
遵循给您优先通行权的那个

text: You're driving behind a farm tractor on a two-lane road. The tractor is moving slowly and pulls partially onto the shoulder to let you pass. What should you consider?
explanation: Even when farm equipment yields partially, you must still ensure the passing zone is safe and legal, checking for oncoming traffic.

choices.text: Pass immediately since the tractor moved over
Check for oncoming traffic before passing, as you would normally
You cannot pass farm equipment on rural roads
Wait for the tractor to completely exit the roadway

translations.text: 您在双向道路上跟在一辆农用拖拉机后面。拖拉机行驶缓慢并部分驶入路肩让您超车。您应该考虑什么？
translations.explanation: 即使农用设备部分让行，您仍必须确保超车区域安全合法，检查对向来车。
choices: 立即超车因为拖拉机让路了
像平常一样检查对向来车后再超车
您不能在乡村道路上超越农用设备
等拖拉机完全驶离车道

text: You're approaching an intersection where you intend to go straight, and you see a vehicle in the oncoming left-turn lane. Their turn signal is on, but they're not moving. How should you proceed?
explanation: Never assume other drivers will complete their intended actions; always be prepared for unexpected behavior and proceed with caution.

choices.text: Assume they will turn and proceed normally
Slow down and be prepared to stop
Flash your lights to signal your approach
Speed up to clear the intersection quickly

translations.text: 您接近交叉路口准备直行，看到对向左转车道有一辆车。他们的转向灯亮着但没有移动。您应该如何通行？
translations.explanation: 永远不要假设其他驾驶员会完成他们预期的动作；总是为意外行为做好准备并谨慎通行。
choices: 假设他们会转弯并正常通行
加速快速通过交叉路口

text: A vehicle is stopped ahead of you with its hazard lights flashing. You can see it's not at an intersection or bus stop. What should you assume about right of way?
explanation: Hazard lights indicate an emergency or dangerous situation, so you should proceed very cautiously and be prepared for unexpected conditions.

choices.text: You have the right of way to pass immediately
The stopped vehicle is yielding the right of way to you
You should proceed with extreme caution as there may be a hazard
The vehicle will move when they see you approaching

translations.text: 前方有车辆停着并闪着危险警示灯。您可以看到它不在交叉路口或公交车站。关于优先通行权您应该假设什么？
translations.explanation: 危险警示灯表示紧急情况或危险状况，您应该极其谨慎地通行并为意外情况做好准备。
choices: 您有立即超车的优先通行权
停着的车辆在向您让行
您应该极其谨慎地通行因为可能有危险
车辆看到您接近时会移动

text: You're turning left from a two-way street onto a one-way street. Traffic approaching from your right on the one-way street is about 100 feet away. What should you do?
explanation: You must yield to oncoming traffic and only turn when you can do so without forcing other vehicles to slow down or change course.

choices.text: Turn quickly before they reach you
Yield until you can turn without causing them to brake or swerve
Turn since you have the right of way when entering

translations.text: 您从双向街道左转进入单向街道。单向街道上从您右侧驶来的车辆大约在100英尺外。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 您必须让行给迎面车辆，只有在不强迫其他车辆减速或改变路线的情况下才能转弯。
choices: 在他们到达前快速转弯
让行直到您可以在不使他们刹车或转向的情况下转弯
转弯因为您进入时有优先通行权

text: At an uncontrolled intersection (no signs or signals), you're turning left and another vehicle approaching from the opposite direction is going straight. Who has the right of way?
explanation: At uncontrolled intersections, straight-through traffic always has the right of way over turning traffic.

choices.text: You have the right of way since you're already turning
The vehicle going straight has the right of way
Whoever arrives first has the right of way
Both vehicles must stop and negotiate

translations.text: 在无控制交叉路口（无标志或信号），您左转，另一辆车从相反方向接近要直行。谁有优先通行权？
translations.explanation: 在无控制交叉路口，直行车辆总是比转弯车辆有优先通行权。
choices: 您有优先通行权因为您已经在转弯
直行车辆有优先通行权
先到达者有优先通行权
两辆车都必须停下协商

text: You're driving in the right lane when you notice a vehicle on the shoulder ahead with a flat tire. What is your legal obligation regarding right of way?
explanation: Many states have 'Move Over' laws requiring drivers to change lanes away from stopped vehicles or significantly reduce speed when passing.

choices.text: Maintain your lane and speed since you have the right of way
Move to the left lane if safely possible, or slow down significantly
Flash your lights to warn other drivers

translations.text: 您在右车道行驶时注意到前方路肩上有辆爆胎的车。关于优先通行权您的法律义务是什么？
translations.explanation: 许多州都有'让路'法律，要求驾驶员在安全情况下远离停车的车道或在通过时显著减速。
choices: 保持车道和车速因为您有优先通行权
如果安全可行就变到左车道，或显著减速

text: You're at a green light waiting to turn left. An ambulance with flashing lights approaches the intersection from your right, also wanting to turn left onto the same street. What should you do?
explanation: When an emergency vehicle needs to use the same path, clear the intersection safely by completing your movement, then immediately pull over to yield.

choices.text: Complete your turn first since you were there first
Clear the intersection by completing your turn, then pull over
Back up to allow the ambulance room
Stop in the intersection until the ambulance passes

translations.text: 您在绿灯时等待左转。一辆闪着灯的救护车从您右侧接近交叉路口，也想左转到同一条街。您应该怎么做？
translations.explanation: 当紧急车辆需要使用同一路径时，通过完成您的行动安全清空交叉路口，然后立即靠边让行。
choices: 先完成转弯因为您先到
通过完成转弯清空交叉路口，然后靠边停车
后退给救护车让出空间
在交叉路口停车直到救护车通过

text: At a four-way intersection with malfunctioning traffic signals, you arrive simultaneously with three other vehicles: one to your right traveling straight, one to your left turning left, and one across from you turning right. Who has the right of way?
explanation: When traffic signals malfunction, the intersection is treated as a four-way stop. The vehicle to your right always has the right of way when arriving simultaneously.

choices.text: You have the right of way
The vehicle across from you turning right
All vehicles must yield to each other and proceed with extreme caution

text: You are making a left turn at an intersection where the opposing traffic light shows a flashing yellow arrow while yours shows a solid green arrow. An ambulance with sirens is approaching from behind at approximately 45 mph in the opposing lane 200 feet away. What should you do?
explanation: Emergency vehicles always have the right of way regardless of traffic signals. You must yield even if you have a green arrow, stopping safely to allow the ambulance to pass.

choices.text: Complete your turn immediately since you have a green arrow
Stop in the intersection and wait for the ambulance to pass
Reverse back to your original position
Accelerate through the turn to clear the intersection quickly

text: At an unmarked intersection where a 35 mph road intersects with a 25 mph residential street, both vehicles arrive at exactly the same time. The vehicle on the residential street is turning left, while the vehicle on the arterial road is going straight. Who yields?
explanation: The vehicle turning left must yield to oncoming traffic going straight, regardless of which road has the higher speed limit. Left-turning vehicles always yield to straight-through traffic at unmarked intersections.

choices.text: The vehicle on the residential street yields to the arterial road
The vehicle going straight yields to the turning vehicle
The vehicle on the arterial road yields because of higher speed limit
Both vehicles have equal right of way and must negotiate

text: You are in a roundabout with a 25 mph speed limit when an emergency vehicle with lights and sirens needs to enter. There are vehicles 50 feet ahead and behind you in the roundabout. What is the correct action?
explanation: You should continue to the next exit and then pull over safely. Stopping within a roundabout creates dangerous conditions for other traffic.

choices.text: Stop immediately in your lane within the roundabout
Exit at the next available exit and pull over
Continue around the roundabout at normal speed
Reverse direction to let the emergency vehicle through

text: At a T-intersection where you are on the through street traveling 40 mph, a vehicle on the terminating street has been waiting to turn left for 15 seconds with their signal on. As you approach within 100 feet, they begin their turn. Who is at fault if a collision occurs?
explanation: Vehicles on terminating streets at T-intersections must yield to all traffic on the through street. The waiting time and turn signal do not override this requirement.

choices.text: You are at fault for not yielding to a signaling vehicle
The turning vehicle is at fault for failing to yield
The driver who was waiting longer has right of way

text: You are approaching a school zone with a 20 mph speed limit at 3:15 PM on a Wednesday, traveling 18 mph. A school bus with flashing red lights is stopped 300 feet ahead in the opposite direction on a four-lane divided highway with a 12-foot median barrier. What must you do?
explanation: On divided highways with barriers of 5 feet or higher, traffic traveling in the opposite direction is not required to stop for school buses. However, traffic in the same direction must stop.

choices.text: Stop until the red lights stop flashing
Continue at current speed since you're on a divided highway
Reduce speed to 15 mph and proceed with caution
Stop only if children are visible

text: At a yield sign where you must merge onto a highway, traffic in the right lane is traveling 65 mph with vehicles spaced approximately 8 seconds apart. You can accelerate to 45 mph in 6 seconds. When should you merge?
explanation: You need adequate space to safely merge and accelerate to highway speed. A 10-second gap provides sufficient safety margin for merging and acceleration.

choices.text: Immediately, since merging traffic has right of way during acceleration
When you can match the speed of highway traffic
When there is at least a 10-second gap between vehicles
Wait for traffic to slow down or change lanes for you

text: You are turning right on red after a complete stop. A pedestrian begins crossing the crosswalk from your right side when they are 25 feet from your lane. The crosswalk is 40 feet wide and the pedestrian appears to be walking at normal speed (3 mph). What should you do?
explanation: Pedestrians always have the right of way in crosswalks. You must wait for pedestrians to completely clear the intersection before proceeding, even when turning right on red.

choices.text: Proceed slowly since the pedestrian has time to cross
Wait for the pedestrian to completely cross the intersection
Sound your horn and proceed with caution
Turn right since you have already stopped

text: At a four-way stop intersection, you arrive first and stop completely. Two seconds later, a vehicle arrives at the stop sign to your right. As you begin to proceed straight, a third vehicle approaches the intersection at 25 mph without stopping from your left. How should you respond?
explanation: Even though you have the right of way, safety requires yielding to avoid a collision with a vehicle that is clearly not going to stop.

choices.text: Continue through since you have established right of way
Stop and yield to the vehicle from the left
Sound your horn and accelerate through the intersection
Yield to the vehicle on your right first

text: You are driving on a two-lane road at 50 mph when you see a funeral procession of 12 vehicles approaching from the opposite direction with headlights on and hazard lights flashing. The lead vehicle displays a funeral flag. What is required by law in most states?
explanation: Most states require drivers to yield right of way to funeral processions by reducing speed and pulling aside, even when traveling in the opposite direction.

choices.text: Continue at normal speed since you're in the opposite direction
Reduce speed to 35 mph and pull to the right shoulder
Stop completely until the entire procession passes
Yield right of way only to the lead vehicle

text: At a railroad crossing with flashing red lights but no crossing gates, you stop 20 feet from the tracks. After waiting 30 seconds with no visible train, the lights continue flashing. A line of 8 cars has formed behind you. What should you do?
explanation: Flashing red lights at railroad crossings have the same authority as a stop sign for trains. You must not cross until the lights stop flashing, regardless of waiting time.

choices.text: Proceed slowly across the tracks after checking both directions
Wait until the lights stop flashing completely
Call the railroad company's emergency number posted at the crossing
Back up and find an alternate route

text: You are in the left lane of a three-lane highway traveling 70 mph when you see an ambulance approaching from behind with lights and sirens. The right and center lanes have heavy traffic moving at 55 mph with gaps of only 2-3 seconds between vehicles. What is the best response?
explanation: When safe to do so, moving to the shoulder allows the emergency vehicle to pass safely without disrupting traffic flow in the travel lanes.

choices.text: Maintain speed and position since there's no safe space to move
Gradually slow down to create a gap for lane changing
Signal and force your way into the center lane immediately
Move onto the shoulder if it's paved and clear

text: At a complex intersection with a traffic light, you have a green arrow for a left turn. Simultaneously, pedestrians have a walk signal for the crosswalk you're turning into, and a cyclist is approaching the intersection against the red light at 15 mph. Who has the priority right of way?
explanation: Pedestrians with a legal walk signal have the right of way over turning vehicles, even those with green arrows. The cyclist running the red light has no legal right of way.

choices.text: You have priority with the green arrow
The pedestrians with the walk signal
The cyclist as a vulnerable road user
All must yield to each other equally

text: You are approaching a flashing yellow traffic light at 35 mph in a 45 mph zone. Cross traffic has a flashing red light. A vehicle from the cross street has been stopped for 10 seconds and begins to proceed as you are 150 feet from the intersection. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Flashing yellow means proceed with caution but maintain right of way. Flashing red means stop and then yield to traffic with the flashing yellow signal.

choices.text: The cross traffic vehicle since they stopped first
You have the right of way with the flashing yellow
Both vehicles must come to a complete stop

text: On a narrow mountain road with 8-foot lanes, you encounter an oversized truck coming toward you. There is a designated turnout 200 feet behind you and another 300 feet ahead. Traffic is backing up behind both vehicles. Who should use the turnout?
explanation: On narrow roads, the vehicle closest to a safe turnout should use it to allow traffic to pass, regardless of vehicle size or direction of travel.

choices.text: The smaller vehicle should always yield
The vehicle closest to a turnout should use it
The vehicle traveling uphill has the right of way
The vehicle with fewer cars behind them should yield

text: At a highway on-ramp, you are accelerating to merge when you notice the vehicle ahead of you in the acceleration lane has stopped due to heavy traffic. You are traveling 40 mph and are 100 feet behind. What is the safest legal action?
explanation: You must stop behind the other vehicle and wait for a safe opportunity to merge. Changing lanes abruptly or using the shoulder illegally creates dangerous conditions.

choices.text: Change lanes into highway traffic immediately
Stop behind the other vehicle and wait
Use the shoulder to continue merging
Sound horn and expect the other vehicle to move

text: You arrive at a stop sign at the same time as a school bus displaying flashing red lights and an extended stop sign. The bus is to your right and you intend to turn right, while the bus is going straight. Children are present. Who proceeds first?
explanation: When a school bus has flashing red lights and extended stop sign, all traffic must remain stopped until the lights stop flashing and children are safely clear.

choices.text: You proceed first since you're turning right
The school bus proceeds first due to its size
Neither vehicle moves until children are clear and bus lights stop flashing
Both vehicles negotiate the intersection together slowly

text: At an intersection controlled by a traffic officer whose signals conflict with the traffic light (officer signals stop while light is green), what should you do when the officer is positioned 40 feet from your vehicle and making direct eye contact?
explanation: Traffic officers' signals always take precedence over traffic lights, signs, and road markings. Their directions must be obeyed immediately.

choices.text: Follow the traffic light since it's the permanent signal
Stop and ask the officer for clarification
Follow whichever signal is more restrictive

text: You are making a U-turn at an intersection where it's legal, and you have yielded to oncoming traffic. As you begin the turn, a pedestrian steps into the crosswalk 30 feet away, and a cyclist approaches from behind at 20 mph in the bike lane. Who has the right of way?
explanation: Pedestrians always have the right of way in crosswalks. Even during legal U-turns, you must yield to pedestrians who have entered the crosswalk.

choices.text: You have the right of way since you started the legal turn
The pedestrian has the right of way in the crosswalk
The cyclist has the right of way in the bike lane
Whoever reaches the conflict point first

text: At a roundabout with a 20 mph speed limit, you are traveling at 15 mph when a motorcycle enters from the next entrance at 25 mph. The motorcycle is now 75 feet behind you in the same lane. What should you do?
explanation: Vehicles already in the roundabout have the right of way over entering traffic. The motorcycle should have yielded before entering and must adjust to your speed.

choices.text: Maintain your speed since you were in the roundabout first
Speed up to match the motorcycle's speed
Signal and exit at the next available exit
Move to the outer edge of your lane

text: You are stopped at a red light in the right lane with your right turn signal on. The light turns green, but before you can turn right, a pedestrian begins crossing from the left side of the crosswalk. The pedestrian is walking slowly and is 35 feet from your lane in a 50-foot crosswalk. What should you do?
explanation: You must wait for pedestrians to completely clear the crosswalk before turning, even when you have a green light. Pedestrians have the right of way in crosswalks.

choices.text: Turn slowly since the pedestrian has time to cross
Wait for the pedestrian to completely cross before turning
Sound your horn to alert the pedestrian to hurry
Turn behind the pedestrian since they've passed your lane

text: On a highway with a 70 mph speed limit, you are traveling in the right lane at 65 mph when you approach a broken-down vehicle on the shoulder with a tow truck displaying flashing amber lights. The left lane has steady traffic at 70 mph with 3-second gaps. What does the Move Over law require?
explanation: Move Over laws require drivers to change lanes away from emergency vehicles when possible, or significantly reduce speed if unable to change lanes safely.

choices.text: Slow down to 55 mph and stay in your lane
Move to the left lane or reduce speed significantly if unable to move over
Stop completely behind the emergency vehicles
Flash your hazard lights and continue at normal speed

text: At a T-intersection where you are turning left from the through street onto the terminating street, you have a green light but no arrow. Oncoming traffic has the same green light. A pedestrian is crossing the terminating street in the crosswalk with a walk signal. Who has the right of way?
explanation: When turning left on a green light without an arrow, you must yield to oncoming traffic first, then to pedestrians in the crosswalk before completing your turn.

choices.text: You have the right of way with the green light
Oncoming traffic has the right of way
The pedestrian has the right of way
All parties must yield to each other

text: You are driving on a rural road at 55 mph when you encounter a horse and rider on the shoulder. The horse appears startled and begins moving toward the roadway. You are 200 feet away with oncoming traffic 400 feet away in the other lane. What should you do?
explanation: Horses can be unpredictable and easily startled. You should slow down significantly and be prepared to stop, as sudden movements or loud noises can cause the horse to bolt into traffic.

choices.text: Maintain speed and sound your horn
Slow down significantly and be prepared to stop
Change lanes to avoid the horse
Speed up to pass before the horse enters the road

text: At a parking lot exit where you must cross a sidewalk to reach the street, a jogger is approaching at 6 mph and is 60 feet away. The sidewalk is busy with pedestrian traffic in both directions. When can you proceed?
explanation: When exiting a driveway or parking lot across a sidewalk, you must yield to all pedestrian traffic. Wait for a clear gap before proceeding to avoid blocking the sidewalk.

choices.text: Proceed slowly since you're exiting private property
Wait for a clear gap in all pedestrian traffic
Sound your horn and proceed with caution
Proceed after yielding only to the approaching jogger

text: At a 4-way stop intersection, Vehicle A arrives at 3:15:12 PM, Vehicle B arrives at 3:15:15 PM from the right of Vehicle A, and Vehicle C arrives at 3:15:17 PM from across Vehicle A. What is the correct order of movement?
explanation: Vehicle A has right-of-way as it arrived first. Vehicle B goes next despite being to the right because it arrived before Vehicle C.

choices.text: Vehicle A, then B, then C
Vehicle B, then A, then C
Vehicle A, then C, then B
Vehicle C, then A, then B

text: When making a left turn at an intersection with a pedestrian countdown timer showing 8 seconds remaining, and an oncoming vehicle is 150 feet away traveling 35 mph, what should you do?
explanation: At 35 mph, the oncoming vehicle covers about 51 feet per second, reaching the intersection in approximately 3 seconds, making the turn unsafe.

choices.text: Proceed if you entered the intersection legally

text: At an intersection where your traffic light has been green for 45 seconds and the total green phase is typically 60 seconds, you're 80 feet from the intersection traveling 25 mph. An emergency vehicle with sirens is approaching from behind. Your action should be:
explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping before the intersection, not within it.

choices.text: Pull to the right before the intersection

text: Two vehicles approach an uncontrolled T-intersection simultaneously. Vehicle A is on the through street traveling 30 mph, Vehicle B is on the terminating street traveling 20 mph and wants to turn right. Distance between vehicles is 120 feet. Who has right-of-way?
explanation: Vehicle A has right-of-way as it's on the through street. Vehicles on terminating streets must yield to through traffic regardless of turning direction.

text: At a roundabout with a 25 mph advisory speed, Vehicle A is already in the roundabout traveling at 20 mph, Vehicle B approaches the yield line at 15 mph wanting to enter. The gap between them will be 75 feet when Vehicle B reaches the entry point. What should Vehicle B do?
explanation: Vehicles already in the roundabout always have right-of-way over entering vehicles, regardless of speed or gap distance.

choices.text: Enter if no other traffic is visible

text: When parallel parking on a street with a bike lane, you notice a cyclist 200 feet behind traveling at approximately 18 mph. Your parking maneuver will take about 25 seconds. Should you proceed?
explanation: At 18 mph, the cyclist covers about 26 feet per second and will reach your location in about 8 seconds, well before you complete the 25-second parking maneuver.

choices.text: Yes, you have right-of-way when parking
No, wait for the cyclist to pass
Yes, but signal for 10 seconds first

text: At a highway on-ramp where the acceleration lane is 1,200 feet long, you're merging at 45 mph while traffic in the right lane is moving at 65 mph with vehicles spaced 300 feet apart. What's the safest approach?
explanation: Merging traffic must adjust speed to safely blend with highway traffic. The 1,200-foot acceleration lane provides adequate distance to reach highway speeds.

choices.text: Maintain 45 mph and merge when safe
Signal and expect traffic to slow down

text: Two school buses approach an intersection from perpendicular directions. Bus A has 32 students and arrives 4 seconds first, Bus B has 28 students. Both have flashing yellow lights (preparing to stop). Who has right-of-way?
explanation: Standard right-of-way rules apply to school buses at intersections. The first to arrive has right-of-way regardless of passenger count.

choices.text: Both must come to complete stop
Whoever reaches the stop sign first

text: At a controlled intersection, the light turns yellow when you're 85 feet away traveling 40 mph. The yellow light duration is 4.5 seconds and the intersection width is 60 feet. What should you do?
